problems.title: Generate All Subsets
problems.prompt: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.
constraints: All the numbers of nums are unique

problems.title: Permutations
problems.prompt: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints: All the integers of nums are unique

problems.title: Generate Parentheses
problems.prompt: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.

problems.title: Combination Sum
problems.prompt: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. You may use the same number from candidates an unlimited number of times. The solution set must not contain duplicate combinations.

problems.title: Word Search Problem
problems.prompt: Given an m x n grid of characters board and a string word, return true if word exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically). The same letter cell may not be used more than once.

problems.title: N-Queens Lite
problems.prompt: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given an integer n, return the number of distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle.

problems.title: N-Queens Placement Lite
problems.prompt: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given an integer n, return all distinct solutions. Each solution contains a distinct board configuration of the n-queens' placement where 'Q' and '.' both indicate a queen and an empty space respectively.

solution.python:
class Solution:
    def solveNQueens(self, n: int) -> list[list[str]]:
        board = [["."] * n for _ in range(n)]
        res = []
        cols = [False] * n
        diag1 = [False] * (2 * n)
        diag2 = [False] * (2 * n)

        def backtrack(row: int) -> None:
            if row == n:
                res.append(["".join(r) for r in board])
                return
            for c in range(n):
                d1 = row - c + n
                d2 = row + c
                if cols[c] or diag1[d1] or diag2[d2]:
                    continue
                cols[c] = diag1[d1] = diag2[d2] = True
                board[row][c] = "Q"
                backtrack(row + 1)
                board[row][c] = "."
                cols[c] = diag1[d1] = diag2[d2] = False

        backtrack(0)
        return res

problems.title: Palindrome Partitioning
problems.prompt: Given a string s, partition s such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitioning of s.
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters
